backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want to define and reuse named audience segments (employees, leadership, clients, press, regulators) with attributes such as role, region, language and seniority, so that every prediction is framed against the actual readers.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Create/edit/delete and reuse named audiences with role/region/language/seniority; selectable per analysis; a default set seeded per organisation; stored per tenant.

backlog.User Story: As a brand manager, I want to codify our tone-of-voice, approved/avoided terminology and core narratives in the platform, so that artifacts are scored against our actual standards rather than generic norms.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Brand-voice attributes, do/don't terminology and messaging pillars captured, versioned and applied during scoring; editable only by Brand Manager/Admin roles.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want to tag each artifact's objective (inform, align, persuade, reassure, drive action), so that the composite score weights the metrics that matter for that intent.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Objective selectable per artifact; metric weighting (attention/emotion/comprehension/memory) changes by objective; objective and weighting shown in the report.

backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want a configurable taxonomy of communication artifact types with engineering artifacts excluded, so that the analyser only ever scores in-scope comms.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Artifact types configurable per tenant; code/engineering documents blocked or flagged out-of-scope; selected type drives which checks run.

backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want one central, versioned library of brand, tone, terminology and compliance rules, so that every analysis draws on a single source of truth.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Versioned rule library; per-artifact-type rule sets; publish/rollback; changes apply org-wide on publish.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager in a global IT firm, I want to analyse comms in English plus priority languages (incl. Hindi/Indic scripts), so that regional audiences are properly served.
backlog.Benchmark Source: TRIBE v2 (zero-shot languages), Poppulo (30+ langs), VisibleThread
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Language auto-detect; analysis in English + configured priority languages; per-language norms; correct Indic-script handling in UI and exports.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want to upload Word, PDF and PowerPoint files, so that I can analyse the artifacts we actually produce.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Parse docx/pptx/pdf (python-docx/python-pptx/PyMuPDF) incl. text, layout and images; slide structure preserved; OCR for scanned PDFs; source files stored in S3/R2-compatible object storage.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want instant text-only scoring for emails and memos, so that everyday comms get feedback without a full analysis run.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Synchronous sub-2s scoring of clarity/tone/brand on pasted/typed text; no GPU/multimodal run required; one-click upgrade to full analysis.

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want to upload town-hall recordings and exec videos, so that spoken communications are analysed too.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Accept mp4/mov/wav/mp3; transcribe via self-hosted Whisper (residency/no-train safe) with timestamps; align transcript to frames; run as an async job with status; media stored in object storage.

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want to analyse a published intranet page or email HTML in context, so that the as-seen version is assessed.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Render URL/HTML to image+DOM; capture above-the-fold; honour auth via connector; snapshot stored; no personal data placed in URLs.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want a deck analysed as a timed viewing sequence rather than just text, so that pacing and flow are assessed.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Deck rendered (LibreOffice headless) to a timed visual+text sequence; per-slide and flow scores; pacing/overload flags; async job.

backlog.User Story: As the platform, I want to auto-detect and exclude engineering artifacts (code, configs, architecture/design docs), so that analysis stays within communication scope.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Classifier flags engineering/code content; excluded from scoring with a stated reason; admin-only override; decision logged.

backlog.Epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
backlog.User Story: As an ML/platform engineer, I want TRIBE v2 served on a decoupled GPU inference tier and called by the app, so that rendered artifacts yield audience-response signals with no model training.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Artifact rendered to TRIBE v2's expected modalities; inference runs on a GPU host (e.g. Modal/Replicate/Baseten), not on Vercel/Render; raw outputs captured per artifact/segment; no in-house training.

backlog.Epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
backlog.User Story: As an architect, I want a model-agnostic interface with TRIBE v2 as primary and other models addable, so that we are never locked to one model or licence.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Common inference interface; route per metric/modality; add/swap/disable models by config; swapping the underlying model requires no application rewrite.

backlog.Epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
backlog.User Story: As an ML/platform engineer, I want raw model signals mapped and calibrated into comms metrics, so that the analyser reports attention/comprehension/memorability, not raw vectors.
backlog.Benchmark Source: TRIBE v2 (raw response proxy), Neurons, DAIVID
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Mapping converts raw outputs to 0-100 comms metrics; calibrated, documented and validated against human ground-truth; norms/embeddings in Postgres+pgvector. This layer is the analyser's core IP.

backlog.Epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
backlog.User Story: As an architect, I want every model's licence checked against our commercial use, so that the analyser is legally shippable.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Per-model licence register; TRIBE v2 (CC BY-NC 4.0) used in production only under a Meta commercial licence, OR restricted to internal/eval with production inference routed to a commercially-usable model; CI gate blocks non-compliant models; legal sign-off recorded.

backlog.Epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
backlog.User Story: As an ML/platform engineer, I want to combine specialised models per metric alongside TRIBE v2, so that each metric uses the best-fit model.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Per-metric model assignment (e.g. saliency for attention, emotion model, NLP for clarity); results merged under one scoring contract; configurable per artifact type; all behind the abstraction layer.

backlog.Epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want scores per slide, paragraph or scene, so that I can see exactly which parts work and which don't.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Per-slide/paragraph/scene scoring; timeline view for audio/video; drill-down to weakest segments.

backlog.Epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
backlog.User Story: As an ML/platform engineer, I want every score stamped with model+adapter+mapping+input hash, so that any result is reproducible and auditable.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Versions recorded on every result; re-run reproduces the score; changelog of model and mapping changes maintained.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want a predicted-attention heatmap over a slide or email, so that I can see what readers notice first.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Saliency heatmap overlay; focus order; area-of-interest scoring; export as PNG.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want to know what lands in the first seconds and above the fold, so that the key message is never missed.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Early-attention score; flag when key message/brand falls outside a high-salience zone; recommendation to reposition.

backlog.User Story: As a brand manager, I want a read on visual hierarchy and clutter on decks, so that slides aren't overloaded.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Clutter/complexity score; competing-focal-point flags; per-slide; benchmarked against strong decks.

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want predicted emotional response (e.g. trust, reassurance, anxiety) to a message, so that sensitive comms land as intended.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Emotion spectrum per artifact/segment with intensity and valence; flags unintended negative emotion; per-segment for AV.

backlog.User Story: As a brand manager, I want detected tone compared to our target tone, so that exec comms feel on-brand.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Tone profile (e.g. confident/empathetic/formal); gap vs target tone; sentence-level tone flags.

backlog.User Story: As a policy officer, I want change, restructuring and crisis comms checked for fear or ambiguous framing, so that we communicate responsibly.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Detects alarming/ambiguous framing; suggests clearer, reassuring alternatives; review recorded.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want delivery cues (pace, emphasis, filler) in exec videos surfaced, so that leaders present with impact.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: From transcript+audio: pace, filler and emphasis cues with suggestions; no biometric data on the speaker stored without consent.

backlog.User Story: As a policy officer, I want readability and plain-language scoring on policies and training, so that employees can actually understand them.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Readability indices; long/passive/ambiguous-sentence flags; jargon list; reading grade vs target.

backlog.User Story: As a training lead, I want a predicted cognitive-load and comprehension score, so that training materials aren't overwhelming.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Cognitive-demand score per section; overload flags; chunking suggestions; benchmarked.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want undefined acronyms and off-list jargon flagged, so that messages are clear to every audience.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Detect undefined/inconsistent acronyms; flag banned jargon against the standards library; suggest plain alternatives.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want to confirm the core message is clearly stated and easy to find, so that the point isn't buried.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Identify the stated key message; score prominence and clarity; flag if buried; suggest a stronger lead.

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want scannability (headings, length, bullets) assessed, so that busy employees can skim.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Structure score; long-block/missing-heading flags; suggested chunking; tuned per channel.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want a predicted memorability score for the key message, so that important comms stick.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Predicted recall for key message and overall; weak-retention flags; second-by-second for AV.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want to see which points are most and least memorable, so that I can reinforce the right ones.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Points ranked by predicted retention; repetition/placement suggestions; exportable.

backlog.Epic: E08 Brand Voice, Tone & Consistency Governance
backlog.User Story: As a brand manager, I want each artifact scored against codified brand voice, so that everything sounds like us.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Brand-alignment score; deviations with reasons and fixes; per-section; trend over time.

backlog.Epic: E08 Brand Voice, Tone & Consistency Governance
backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want consistency checks across a campaign or document set, so that messaging doesn't contradict itself.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Cross-document terminology/claims consistency; conflict flags; campaign-level view.

backlog.Epic: E08 Brand Voice, Tone & Consistency Governance
backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want live brand and clarity guidance while I write in Word/PowerPoint, so that I fix issues at the source.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Office sidebar flags brand/tone/clarity inline; one-click fixes; score updates live.

backlog.Epic: E08 Brand Voice, Tone & Consistency Governance
backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want AI-generated drafts scored and guardrailed before they enter comms, so that AI content stays on-brand and compliant.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Scores LLM output against the standards library; blocks off-brand/non-compliant content; offers fixes; action logged.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want one headline effectiveness score, so that I can judge readiness at a glance.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Weighted composite 0-100 with attention/emotion/comprehension/memory/brand sub-scores; objective-weighted; pass/needs-work threshold.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want benchmarks against our own best comms and artifact-type norms, so that scores are meaningful.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Percentile vs org history and artifact-type norm; top-quartile threshold; norms store grows with usage (pgvector).

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want to compare two versions side by side, so that I pick the stronger one before sending.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Side-by-side scores and deltas; highlights what changed; recommends a winner with reasons.

backlog.User Story: As a brand manager, I want to benchmark our public comms against peers, so that we stand out.
backlog.Benchmark Source: Dragonfly (Chrome ext competitor analysis), System1 league tables
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Scores public peer comms; relative positioning and gap insights; respects copyright/ToS.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want specific recommendations for weak segments, so that I know how to fix them, not just what's wrong.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Prioritised fixes per weakness with expected uplift and rationale; accept/dismiss.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want each recommendation to cite its driver, so that I can trust and act on the advice.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Each recommendation links to the metric and segment evidence behind it; expected uplift shown; accept/track.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want on-brand AI rewrites of weak passages, so that I can improve copy fast without losing voice.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Rewrites honour brand voice and compliance rules; before/after predicted score shown; human approval required before use.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want to preview the predicted score if I accept changes, so that I can justify edits.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Edited draft re-scored on demand; uplift vs baseline shown; decision logged.

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want guidance tuned to the channel (email, intranet, town-hall deck, press release), so that advice fits the medium.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Channel profiles with format rules; channel-aware scoring and tips; subject-line/preview checks for email.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want subject lines and opening hooks scored and improved, so that messages get opened and read.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Generate and score subject/hook variants; predicted open/read; select and insert.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want the analyser inside Word and PowerPoint, so that I can analyse without leaving my tools.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Office.js add-ins analyse the current doc/deck; inline results; SSO; works in the client's M365 tenant.

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want pre-send analysis in Outlook and Teams, so that internal messages are checked before they go out.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Office.js add-ins run a pre-send check; warn or block on low score (configurable); one-click full report.

backlog.User Story: As a platform engineer at the client, I want a documented API and SDK, so that we can embed analysis into our own workflows and DAM.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: REST API with OpenAPI spec + SDK; API-key auth; rate limits; webhooks for async results; versioned docs.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want draft->review->approve stages with sign-off, so that exec comms are governed.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Configurable stages; reviewers; comments; sign-off; status tracked; full audit trail.

backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want minimum scores per artifact type before publish, so that sub-standard comms are caught.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Configurable thresholds; block or warn; override requires a logged reason; per artifact type.

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want to comment on and assign fixes within an analysed artifact, so that the team improves it together.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Threaded comments; assign and resolve; @mentions; notifications.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want a shareable analysis report, so that I can brief stakeholders.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Branded PDF/HTML report (scores, heatmaps, recommendations); access-controlled share link; export stored in object storage.

backlog.User Story: As an owner, I want an org-level dashboard of comms effectiveness over time, so that I can manage the programme on data.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Trends by score, channel, team and artifact type; cohort views; export.

backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want per-team and per-author quality trends, so that I can target coaching.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Scorecards per team/author; improvement trend; privacy-aware aggregation (no surveillance framing).

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want a predicted open/read estimate before sending, so that I can improve reach.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Predicted open/read range vs norms with drivers; improve-then-recheck loop.

backlog.User Story: As an owner, I want predicted scores correlated with actual engagement, so that I can prove value.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Joins predicted scores to downstream engagement (opens/read/sentiment); correlation report with stated caveats.

backlog.User Story: As a policy officer, I want inclusive-language checks on all comms, so that we avoid biased or exclusionary wording.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Flags non-inclusive terms with alternatives; configurable per region/culture.

backlog.User Story: As a policy officer, I want risky or over-promising language flagged in press releases and policies, so that we limit legal and regulatory exposure.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Detects unsupported claims/risk phrases; tied to the policy library; routed to a review queue.

backlog.User Story: As a compliance officer, I want lawful processing of any personal data in artifacts, so that we meet DPDP 2023 and GDPR.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Processing basis/consent captured; PII detection with redaction option; data-subject-rights support; configurable retention.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want to see why a score is what it is, so that I trust and act on it.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Drivers per score; segment attributions; plain-language explanation; no black box.

backlog.User Story: As an ML/platform engineer, I want continuous validation against human ground-truth, so that we can state accuracy credibly.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Held-out human tests; accuracy reported per metric; drift monitoring; published methodology.

backlog.User Story: As a policy officer, I want the models checked for bias across languages and regions, so that predictions are fair.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Bias tests across segments; disparity reporting; mitigation log; per-model model card.

backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want model documentation and human-review controls, so that AI outputs are governed.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Model card (scope/data/limits) per model; human review required for high-stakes outputs; overrides logged.

backlog.User Story: As a compliance officer, I want an append-only log of analyses, gate overrides and AI rewrites, so that governance is provable.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Append-only audit (actor/time/artifact/action/override reason); exportable; no PII in logs.

backlog.Epic: E17 Security, Privacy, IP & Data Residency
backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want SSO and role-based access, so that only authorised staff see sensitive comms.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: SAML/OIDC SSO and SCIM provisioning (e.g. via WorkOS/Entra/Okta); granular roles; access audited.

backlog.Epic: E17 Security, Privacy, IP & Data Residency
backlog.User Story: As a security engineer, I want India/EU data residency and tenant isolation, so that client data stays compliant and separate.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Region-pinned storage and processing (India and/or EU per tenant); per-tenant isolation; AES-256 at rest, TLS1.2+ in transit; managed keys.

backlog.Epic: E17 Security, Privacy, IP & Data Residency
backlog.User Story: As a security engineer at a regulated client, I want a private or on-prem deployment, so that confidential exec and board comms never leave our perimeter.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Containerised (Docker) images deployable to client VPC/Kubernetes/air-gapped; no data egress; customer-managed keys; documented runbook.

backlog.Epic: E17 Security, Privacy, IP & Data Residency
backlog.User Story: As an owner, I want a contractual and technical no-training-on-our-content guarantee, so that confidential comms aren't used to train shared models.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Zero-retention/no-train option; per-tenant model boundaries; enforced and auditable; reflected in contract.

backlog.Epic: E17 Security, Privacy, IP & Data Residency
backlog.User Story: As a security engineer, I want PII in uploaded comms detected and optionally redacted, so that sensitive data is protected during analysis.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Detect PII; redact/mask option; configurable per tenant; action logged; PII never written to logs.

backlog.User Story: As an architect, I want autoscaling, decoupled GPU inference, so that batch audits and peak loads are handled without throttling the app tier.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: GPU inference autoscaled on a dedicated tier (off Vercel/Render); queue + batch; latency SLA targets; per-analysis cost tracked and capped.

backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want to queue large back-catalogue audits, so that we can baseline existing comms.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Bulk job queue (Redis-backed) on background workers; progress/status; partial results; completion notification.

backlog.User Story: As an architect, I want multi-tenant isolation with per-tenant config, so that we can serve multiple IT-services clients securely from one codebase.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Tenant isolation; per-tenant brand/audience/standards config; usage metering; admin separation; same container images usable for single-tenant/on-prem.

backlog.User Story: As an engineer, I want monitoring, alerting and audit logging, so that the service is reliable and auditable.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Metrics/logs/traces (OpenTelemetry/Sentry); uptime SLO with alerting; append-only audit of actions; no PII in telemetry.

backlog.User Story: As an architect, I want a pluggable connector framework (M365, Google, DAM, CMS), so that the analyser fits existing comms stacks.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Pluggable connectors with per-connector auth and permissions; SharePoint/Teams/Drive first; certified M365/Google.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Attention heatmaps, cognitive demand, clarity, engagement, memory, emotion (95%+ vs eye-tracking)
competitors.Enterprise / Comms Fit: Fortune-500 incl. tech; UX/website/asset pre-testing — adjacent to decks & comms
competitors.Signature strength / signal: World-leading consumer-neuroscience suite (Predict / Explore / Research); Copilot recommendations; Memory score.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Synthetic attention + emotion (facial-coding trained); view-through prediction; recommendations
competitors.Signature strength / signal: 17M+ human webcam tests; AttentionX with CreativeX; 20+ patents; Mars sales-lift validation.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: 39 emotions, attention, brand recall, purchase intent, /10 effectiveness; second-by-second; API
competitors.Enterprise / Comms Fit: Creative effectiveness for brands & agencies (Nike, WPP, Snapchat)
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Tens of millions of human responses; Creative Data Feed API; gen-AI creative QA.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Saliency/attention + memorability, comprehension, persuasion, emotion; real-time; Chrome ext
competitors.Enterprise / Comms Fit: CPG/retail creative; competitor analysis via extension
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Patented biological algorithm (QMUL); 89% MIT benchmark; Coca-Cola, GSK, Diageo, Reckitt.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Predictive eye-tracking heatmaps, focus maps, clarity score (90-94% accuracy)
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Deep-learning eye-tracking prediction at the design stage; pre-launch heatmaps.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Predictive attention / eye-tracking AI; saliency scoring
competitors.Signature strength / signal: EU attention-prediction API rooted in cognitive science; fast saliency.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Audience-specific visual performance score (Audience Lens); benchmarks
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Audience-tuned visual scoring; learns from public engagement signals.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Webcam eye-tracking + Attention Predictor AI (VAI, speed, reach, hold)
competitors.Signature strength / signal: 1.7M+ eye-tracking sessions; webcam panels plus synthetic predictor.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Emotional response (Test Your Ad), Star/Spike, + new predictive AI (beta)
competitors.Signature strength / signal: 100,000-ad emotional-norms database; predictive AI layer added 2026.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Consumer-neuroscience (EEG/biometric) + Realeyes attention integration
competitors.Signature strength / signal: NeuroFocus heritage; integrated Realeyes attention (2024); 350B+ frames.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: LINK+/LINK AI copy testing + synthetic (AI) pre-testing from norms DB
competitors.Signature strength / signal: One of the largest creative-testing norms databases; AI synthetic predictions.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Emotion AI / facial & vocal expression analysis (Media Analytics)
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Affectiva emotion-AI pioneer; facial coding at scale; large emotion dataset.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Eye-tracking hardware + attention software/analytics (ground-truth gaze)
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Eye-tracking category leader; ground-truth data that trains predictive models.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: NeuroLab (implicit, eye-tracking, emotion) + AI predictions
competitors.Signature strength / signal: All-in-one neuromarketing research suite with predictive add-ons.

competitors.Primary Archetype: C. Enterprise Content Governance, Brand Voice & Clarity
competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Brand voice, tone, clarity, terminology, inclusivity, compliance scoring + AI guardrails
competitors.Enterprise / Comms Fit: ENTERPRISE content governance — Microsoft, Adobe, Siemens
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Only enterprise-class content-governance platform; 50+ authoring integrations; DFKI origin; ISO 27001/27701.

competitors.Primary Archetype: C. Enterprise Content Governance, Brand Voice & Clarity
competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Brand/style guardrails, terminology, compliance over generative AI
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Full-stack enterprise LLM + brand guardrails; governs human and AI content.

competitors.Primary Archetype: C. Enterprise Content Governance, Brand Voice & Clarity
competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Inclusive language, tone, bias detection, augmented writing
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Augmented-writing pioneer; inclusivity + tone for people-facing comms.

competitors.Primary Archetype: C. Enterprise Content Governance, Brand Voice & Clarity
competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Readability, plain-language, passive/long/ambiguous, risky-language, consistency
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Language/clarity auditing; plain-language compliance; deterministic; SCIF-ready deployment.

competitors.Primary Archetype: C. Enterprise Content Governance, Brand Voice & Clarity
competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Writing quality, tone detection, clarity, brand tones at scale
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Ubiquitous assistant; enterprise tone + style + security; huge install base.

competitors.Primary Archetype: C. Enterprise Content Governance, Brand Voice & Clarity
competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Brand voice controls + marketing content generation/optimization
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Marketing-focused GenAI with brand-voice controls and campaign workflows.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Motivation AI: emotion ontology, message-element scoring, performance prediction
competitors.Enterprise / Comms Fit: Regulated enterprises (banks, retail) — JPMorgan, Verizon, M&S
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Motivation AI; 100k+ scored message elements; 1M+ A/B tests; emotion-to-outcome causal mapping; auditable.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: AI brand-language generation + dynamic optimization; multilingual
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Brand-safe language generation; multilingual; real-time campaign optimization.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Predictive performance score per copy variant; audience targeting
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Predictive copywriting score trained on performance data.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Reach, open/read time, click maps, sentiment, AI personalization & next-best-message
competitors.Enterprise / Comms Fit: ENTERPRISE internal comms — 4,500 orgs, 40+ Fortune 100; India (Bengaluru) office
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Leading employee-comms + digital-signage suite; rich multichannel analytics; AI 'predictable outcomes'.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Engagement heatmaps, sentiment checks, campaign analytics (incl. frontline)
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Multichannel employee-comms cloud; analytics beyond opens; strong frontline reach.

competitors.Primary Archetype: Predicts audience attention / emotion / memory directly from content using AI trained on human (eye-tracking, facial-coding, neuro) data — no live panel. Closest analogue to a TRIBE v2 engine. Built mostly for ADS & consumer creative, not internal/exec/policy comms.
Tests creative against large human-response norms databases, increasingly with AI 'synthetic' prediction. Strong validation heritage; advertising-centric; slower/heavier than pure-AI tools.

competitors.Vendor: C. Enterprise Content Governance, Brand Voice & Clarity
competitors.Primary Archetype: Governs WRITTEN content at enterprise scale — tone, brand voice, terminology, clarity/readability, inclusivity, compliance — with authoring-tool integrations and quality gates. Owns the enterprise relationship and compliance; NO neuro-prediction.
Generates and optimizes the wording of messages and predicts their performance (clicks/conversions) from campaign data. Marketing/outbound-centric; not audience-cognition modelling.

Delivers and MEASURES internal communications across email/intranet/mobile/signage — reach, open/read, sentiment. Strong on distribution & post-hoc analytics; weak on pre-send prediction of cognitive/emotional response.

readme: Enterprise Communications Neuro-Analyzer — Product Strategy & Competitive Backlog
TRIBE v2-class audience-response analyzer for IT services organizations | Big Four strategy advisory draft (v1.1)
Prepared for

An Indian startup client building a neuro-predictive communications analyzer. The product ingests an organization's communication artifacts — official communications, presentations, press releases, internal communications, executive communications, training materials, policies and other comms collateral (engineering artifacts explicitly excluded) — and predicts how the intended audience will respond (attention, emotion, comprehension, memorability, brand fit). Target buyers are large IT services firms such as Sopra Steria, Capgemini and Accenture.
What TRIBE v2 is

TRIBE v2 is Meta FAIR's tri-modal (video + audio + language) foundation model that predicts human brain (fMRI) responses to stimuli — a 'digital twin' of group-average neural activity. The client's product is an ANALYSER that consumes TRIBE v2 (and other open models as needed) — it does NOT train its own model. LICENCE FLAG: TRIBE v2 is released under CC BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial). Using open models is the right approach, but running production inference on TRIBE v2 inside a sold product is a commercial use of a non-commercial model. Resolution does not require building a model — see 'Model selection & licensing fit' below.
What this is

A consolidated, structured product backlog (Platform -> Epic -> Functionality -> User Story) for an ANALYSER that orchestrates open models (TRIBE v2 primary, others where they fit better) and turns their raw output into communication insight. Synthesised from a scan of 25 leading vendors across five competitive archetypes plus market research. Same structure as the sample backlog provided. Use it to scope an MVP, sequence a roadmap, and brief engineering.
Vendors analysed (25)

Neurons, Realeyes, DAIVID, Dragonfly AI, Attention Insight, Expoze.io, Vizit, RealEye, System1, Nielsen, Kantar, Smart Eye (Affectiva), Tobii, CoolTool, Acrolinx (Markup AI), Writer, Textio, VisibleThread, Grammarly, Jasper, Persado, Jacquard (formerly Phrasee), Anyword, Poppulo, Staffbase. See 'Competitor Landscape' sheet. Also-track: PoliteMail, ContactMonkey, Firstup, Cerkl (internal-comms email analytics).
Five archetypes

A. Neuro-Predictive Attention & Emotion AI  |  B. Creative & Ad Effectiveness Testing  |  C. Enterprise Content Governance, Brand Voice & Clarity  |  D. Message & Language Performance Optimization  |  E. Internal Comms Measurement & Engagement. No single incumbent does all five for enterprise internal/exec comms — that white space is the opportunity. Archetype A is the closest direct analogue to a TRIBE v2 engine; Archetype C is where the enterprise buyer relationship and compliance moat already live.
How to use

Filter the 'Backlog' sheet by Platform / Epic / Type / Priority / Phase. 'Epic Summary' pivots story counts by Platform and Phase. 'Competitor Landscape' shows which vendors validate each capability (Benchmark Source column).
COLUMN GUIDE
Platform

Where the story lives: Communications Studio (Web App) | Authoring Add-ins & Integrations | Neuro-Prediction Engine (Core ML) | Analytics & Insights Console | Admin & Governance Console | Platform / Shared Backend.
Type
Foundation = infra/compliance/model the platform needs  |  Parity = table-stakes to match competitors  |  Differentiator = an edge over the field / value driver.
Priority
MoSCoW: Must / Should / Could.
Benchmark Source
Vendor(s) / market evidence that validate the capability.
Phase

Phase 1 (Pilot/MVP) = credible analyzer: text + slide scoring, clarity/tone/brand/attention, Office add-in, single client pilot.  |  Phase 2 (Scale) = multimodal (audio/video), benchmarking, dashboards, workflow gates, integrations, multi-tenant.  |  Phase 3 (Advanced) = full neuro-prediction (emotion/memory, second-by-second), generative rewrite, predictive A/B, competitive benchmarking, on-prem/air-gapped.
Market Coverage
How common the capability is across the 25 vendors: Universal (almost all serious players) | Common (many) | Niche (a few / differentiating).
PRD Ref
Stable cross-reference to the Functional Requirement ID (FR-Exx-n) in the companion Product Requirements Document, so the backlog and the PRD/spec stay in lockstep for the BMAD / Spec-Kit pipeline.
STRATEGY NOTES
Model selection & licensing fit (critical)

The client uses open models, not a trained-in-house model. The job is to SELECT and ORCHESTRATE them. Keep the analyser model-agnostic (story TCA-014) so models can be swapped. For TRIBE v2's non-commercial licence there are two product-safe paths that do NOT involve building a model: (1) secure a commercial licence from Meta for TRIBE v2; or (2) use TRIBE v2 for internal R&D / evaluation only and route PRODUCTION inference to a commercially-usable model (open or commercial API). Per-model licence review + legal sign-off is a Phase-1 'Must' (story TCA-016). Always verify current licence terms directly with Meta, as they can change.
Where the value lives (the analyser's IP)

Because the models are open/commodity, the defensible product is NOT the model — it is the application layer: (a) rendering enterprise artifacts (policies, decks, town-hall videos) into each model's expected input; (b) MAPPING raw model outputs (fMRI/saliency/emotion signals) to actionable comms metrics — attention, comprehension, memorability, brand fit — and calibrating/validating them against human ground-truth (story TCA-015); (c) the enterprise workflow, governance, compliance, integrations and benchmarks. Two analysers using the same open model will differ entirely on this layer.
Where the moat is

Incumbents split into two camps: neuro/attention vendors (Archetype A/B) are strong on prediction but built for ADS and consumer creative, not internal/exec comms or compliance; content-governance vendors (Archetype C, e.g. Acrolinx) own the enterprise relationship and compliance but have NO neuro-prediction. The wedge for an open-model analyser: a neuro-grounded analyser purpose-built for enterprise internal/exec/policy comms, with governance, compliance (DPDP/GDPR), IP protection and on-prem options the consumer-ad vendors don't offer. The model is borrowed; the comms-specific mapping, workflow and trust are owned. Land via the comms/exec office, expand to L&D, policy and brand.
Buyer & GTM

Primary buyers: Head of Internal/Corporate Comms, CEO/Exec office, CHRO/L&D, Brand. For IT-services targets (Sopra Steria, Capgemini, Accenture), confidential exec/board comms make data residency, no-training guarantees and on-prem/air-gapped deployment decisive (stories TCA-055/056/057). Pricing likely enterprise/seat + volume, six-figure annual (cf. Persado, Poppulo).
What changed in v1.1

All 76 user stories revised: tightened wording; acceptance criteria sharpened and made testable; the agreed reference technology stack baked into the relevant criteria; and any 'build/train a model' language removed throughout (the product orchestrates open models only). Added the 'PRD Ref' column linking each story to its FR-Exx-n requirement. Epics, types, priorities, phases and benchmark sources are unchanged.
Reference technology stack

Frontend: Next.js + TypeScript on Vercel (Tailwind + shadcn/ui). App/API + orchestration: Python FastAPI on Render. Async work (transcription, batch audits): Render background workers + Redis queue. Data: Render Postgres + pgvector; object storage on S3/Cloudflare R2 (not native to Render). GPU model inference (TRIBE v2 + specialised models): a DECOUPLED GPU tier (e.g. Modal/Replicate/Baseten) — neither Vercel nor Render hosts the model. ASR: self-hosted Whisper. SSO/SCIM: WorkOS-class. Containerise everything (Docker) from day one so the same images run on Render now and on a client's Kubernetes / on-prem later (required by stories TCA-069/074). Note: confirm India data-residency regions with both PaaS providers — they may require an India-region cloud for the data tier.
Compliance is table-stakes

Birth-of-trust issues: analyzing internal/exec comms means handling confidential and personal data — DPDP 2023 (India) and GDPR consent, PII detection/redaction, audit logging, and a contractual no-training-on-our-content guarantee are Phase-1 'Must', not later.
Caveat

Indicative draft for discussion. The product is an analyser that orchestrates open models (TRIBE v2 primary); no model is trained in-house. Vendor facts are from public sources (vendor sites, press, analyst notes) as of mid-2026 and may have changed (e.g. Acrolinx is rebranding to Markup AI; Phrasee is now Jacquard). Validate scope, effort, model licensing and accuracy claims with engineering and legal before commitment. Neuro-prediction outputs should be positioned as decision support, not guaranteed outcomes.
